Most info from [Rock Climb Marvin's Ear, Northwest Region (mountainproject.com)](https://www.mountainproject.com/route/120245974/marvins-ear)

## Difficulty distribution

5.05.95.10P3 - 100'P1 - 100'
P4 - 115'P2 - 90'
P5 - 100'
P6 - 115'
P7 - 100'

## Gear

- 70m rope (needed for rappel)
- 11 quickdraws (3-4 alpine)
- Walkie talkies?

## Approach

This tower is BEFORE (to the right of) Mile High Club's tower.

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/aaj-13201214561-1521826659.ItpaWaHM-667x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 1 - 5.9, 100', 4 bolts

Scramble up to high first bolt with fun stemming on mostly good rock up high.

![Leading P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/120246003_large_1612047223.9Yclkrd-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 2 - 5.10a, 90', 6 bolts

Edges and some slab moves more or less straight up from the belay. Mind the loose rock at end of pitch.

![End of P2](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/120246006_large_1612047299.4A96H9Xe-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 3 - 5.0, 100', 1 bolt

Walk/scramble on ledges. Clip one bolt and scramble towards the anchor.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/120246009_large_1612047395.wLcTWWL-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:4,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:317,&quot;y&quot;:438,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:543,&quot;y&quot;:948},{&quot;x&quot;:469,&quot;y&quot;:658},{&quot;x&quot;:359,&quot;y&quot;:523},{&quot;x&quot;:320,&quot;y&quot;:446}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 4 - 5.9, 115', 9-10 bolts

Follow the bolts up the broad ridge

![End of P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/120246013_large_1612047463.4wcZTcuO-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 5 - 10-, 100', 10 bolts

Follow bolts trending left towards the end of the pitch. **Move past the first set of anchors (rap anchors)** to the next set of bolts farther left. This will set you up the belay the following pitch. 100 feet (10 bolts)

## Pitch 6 - 10b, 115', 9 bolts

Cruxy move over bulge leads to another cruxy section. Rope drag was an issue, but could be managed by extending a few draws.

## Pitch 7 - 10-, 100', 9 bolts

Cool **traverse left** then up through a fun corner and to the anchor just below the summit.

![Airy traverse at start](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2021/05/120246021_large_1612047558.9WfY-1024x768.jpg)

## Scramble to summit

Short scramble to the summit. Most will want to stay on belay for this section.

## Descent
